worlds.description: A drowned nobleman's estate where flooded corridors connect storage vaults crammed with centuries of hoarded possessions—furniture, trunks, rotting textiles stacked to the waterline. The family's portrait collection remains above the flood, and each painting's subject has become a malevolent spirit that whispers to anyone wading past, bargaining secrets for freedom.

A transitional zone where dense living forest abruptly borders a forsaken ash desert, the demarcation unnaturally sharp. Pilgrims navigate this boundary by solving light reflection puzzles—angling ancient mirrors to project sunlight patterns that reveal safe paths through ash storms and expose the hungry things hiding in the tree line's shadow.

Rusted clockwork automatons patrol the salt marshes surrounding a long-abandoned mining operation, still following centuries-old directives to protect explosive-laden tunnels from trespassers. The brackish water has corroded their joints into shrieking motion, and unstable charges deep in the flooded shafts detonate unpredictably, geysering brine and shrapnel.

A cult settlement along a subterranean river where every structure is assembled from identical prefabricated stone modules—the same room repeated hundreds of times in nauseating uniformity. The humid air carries swamp rot from where the river surfaces into putrid marshland, and devotees believe architectural sameness brings them closer to a god of perfect emptiness.

The drowned plazas and flooded boulevards of an ancient underground city, accessible only through water-filled passages that require breath-holding dives between air pockets. Above the waterline, the city's remaining architecture juts from still black pools beneath a cavern ceiling, the whole realm suffused with an oppressive, dreary silence.

A fractured territory where dark forest, cracked desert canyon, and dry wasteland exist in impossible adjacency—walking a hundred paces can transition between all three. No climate logic applies; the land seems stitched together from incompatible remnants of different worlds, and survivors eke out existence in the seams where biomes violently overlap.

Peaceful pastoral farmland where generations of families have unknowingly lived above a labyrinth of hidden passages. Light reflection puzzles guard the entrances—sunlight must strike specific stones at specific angles to reveal trapdoors in root cellars and barn floors. Below, something patient has been listening to footsteps overhead for centuries, mapping the routines of those who till the soil.
